freelance-tax-calculator.seoDescription: Calculate your freelance taxes for 2024. Instantly see your self-employment tax, federal income tax, effective rate, and quarterly estimated payments. Built for US freelancers.
freelance-tax-calculator.seoKeywords: freelance tax calculator, self-employment tax calculator, 1099 tax calculator, quarterly tax calculator, estimated tax payments 2024, independent contractor tax, gig worker tax calculator, freelancer taxes USA
freelance-tax-calculator.seoH1: Freelance Tax Calculator 2024: Self-Employment & Quarterly Taxes

faq.question: What percentage of my freelance income should I set aside for taxes?
faq.answer: A safe rule of thumb is to set aside 25-30% of every payment you receive. This covers both self-employment tax (15.3%) and federal income tax. State taxes vary.

faq.question: Do I need to pay quarterly taxes as a freelancer?
faq.answer: Yes, if you expect to owe $1,000 or more in taxes for the year, the IRS requires quarterly estimated tax payments. Failure to pay can result in an underpayment penalty.

faq.question: What is Form 1099-NEC?
faq.answer: Clients who paid you $600 or more during the year must send you a Form 1099-NEC (formerly 1099-MISC). You report this income on Schedule C of your tax return.

faq.question: Can I deduct my home office as a freelancer?
faq.answer: Yes, but the space must be used regularly and exclusively for business. You can deduct a percentage of rent/mortgage and utilities proportional to the office's square footage.

faq.question: What is the self-employment tax rate?
faq.answer: The self-employment tax rate is 15.3% — 12.4% for Social Security and 2.9% for Medicare. The Social Security portion only applies to the first $168,600 of net self-employment income (2024 limit).

freelance-tax-calculator.content:
Understanding Freelance Taxes in the USA

Freelancers and independent contractors are responsible for paying both the employee and employer portions of Social Security and Medicare taxes — collectively called the Self-Employment (SE) Tax. This currently totals 15.3% (12.4% Social Security + 2.9% Medicare) on net self-employment income.

The Self-Employment Tax Deduction

You can deduct 50% of your self-employment tax from your gross income before calculating income tax. This partially offsets the burden compared to traditional employees.

Quarterly Estimated Tax Payments

Freelancers must pay taxes quarterly (April 15, June 15, September 15, January 15) or face IRS underpayment penalties. Each quarterly payment should be approximately 25% of your estimated annual tax liability.

Key Business Deductions to Track

  • Home office (dedicated workspace)
  • Equipment and software
  • Health insurance premiums
  • Business travel and mileage
  • Professional development and subscriptions
  • Retirement contributions (SEP-IRA, Solo 401k)

tdee-calculator.name: TDEE Calculator
tdee-calculator.description: Calculate your Total Daily Energy Expenditure to know exactly how many calories to eat for your goals.
tdee-calculator.seoTitle: [Gratis] TDEE Calculator (Free) — Total Daily Energy Expenditure & Calorie Calculator
tdee-calculator.seoDescription: Calculate your TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) based on age, weight, height, and activity level. Find your maintenance calories, caloric deficit for weight loss, and surplus for muscle gain.
tdee-calculator.seoKeywords: TDEE calculator, total daily energy expenditure, maintenance calories, calorie calculator, BMR calculator, weight loss calories, calorie deficit calculator, how many calories should I eat, TDEE weight loss
tdee-calculator.seoH1: TDEE Calculator: Find Your Total Daily Calorie Needs

faq.question: How accurate is a TDEE calculator?
faq.answer: TDEE calculators give a solid estimate, typically accurate within 10-15%. Track your calories and weight for 2-3 weeks and adjust based on real-world results.

faq.question: What activity level should I choose?
faq.answer: Most people overestimate their activity level. Unless you have a physical job or exercise intensively 5+ days a week, choose 'sedentary' or 'lightly active' to avoid overestimating your calorie needs.

faq.question: How many calories should I eat to lose 1 pound per week?
faq.answer: Eat 500 calories below your TDEE each day. Since 3,500 calories equals approximately 1 pound of fat, a 500-calorie daily deficit equals about 1 pound lost per week.

faq.question: Why is my weight not changing even though I'm eating at my TDEE?
faq.answer: Your calculated TDEE may be higher than your actual TDEE. Try reducing your intake by 10% and track for another 2-3 weeks. Real-world tracking is more reliable than calculator estimates.

faq.question: Should I eat less on rest days?
faq.answer: For most people, the difference is minimal. Unless you're an advanced athlete, eating a consistent amount daily is psychologically easier and metabolically equivalent to cycling calories.

tdee-calculator.content:
What is TDEE?

Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) is the total number of calories your body burns in a 24-hour period, accounting for everything from basic biological functions to exercise and daily activities. It is the most important number in any body composition plan.

TDEE vs BMR

Your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) is the calories your body burns at complete rest. Your TDEE multiplies your BMR by an activity factor. Our calculator uses the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, considered the most accurate formula for most people.

Using TDEE for Your Goals

  • Weight Loss: Create a 500-calorie daily deficit (eat TDEE minus 500). This creates approximately 1 pound of fat loss per week.
  • Muscle Gain: Eat in a 200-300 calorie surplus above TDEE to minimize fat gain while building muscle.
  • Maintenance: Eat at your TDEE. Your weight should stay stable over time.

pregnancy-due-date-calculator.name: Pregnancy Due Date Calculator
pregnancy-due-date-calculator.description: Calculate your pregnancy due date, trimester dates, and key milestones from your LMP.
pregnancy-due-date-calculator.seoTitle: [Gratis] Pregnancy Due Date Calculator (Free) — Calculate Your Baby's Arrival Date
pregnancy-due-date-calculator.seoDescription: Calculate your pregnancy due date instantly based on your last menstrual period (LMP) or conception date. See all trimester dates, gestational age, and key milestones.
pregnancy-due-date-calculator.seoKeywords: pregnancy due date calculator, due date calculator, LMP calculator, when is my baby due, pregnancy calendar, gestational age calculator, trimester calculator, conception date calculator, baby due date
pregnancy-due-date-calculator.seoH1: Pregnancy Due Date Calculator: Know When Your Baby Arrives

faq.question: How accurate is a due date calculated from LMP?
faq.answer: Within 2-4 days for most women with regular cycles. An early ultrasound (before 14 weeks) provides the most accurate dating. Your healthcare provider may adjust the due date based on ultrasound measurements.

faq.question: What if my periods are irregular?
faq.answer: LMP-based calculations are less accurate with irregular cycles. An early ultrasound measuring the fetal crown-rump length (CRL) is the most accurate way to establish your due date.

faq.question: What is gestational age vs fetal age?
faq.answer: Gestational age is counted from the LMP and is what doctors use. Fetal age (conception age) is approximately 2 weeks less. Our calculator displays gestational age, as used medically.

faq.question: What week is a baby considered full-term?
faq.answer: Full-term is 39-40 weeks. 'Early term' is 37-38 weeks, 'late term' is 41 weeks, and 'post-term' is 42+ weeks. The goal is to reach 39 weeks before delivery for the best outcomes.

faq.question: Can I get an accurate due date from a home pregnancy test?
faq.answer: No, home tests only confirm pregnancy. Due date calculation requires knowing your LMP or using an ultrasound. The test does not indicate how far along you are.

pregnancy-due-date-calculator.content:
How is a Pregnancy Due Date Calculated?

The standard method for calculating a pregnancy due date is Naegele's Rule: add 280 days (40 weeks) to the first day of your Last Menstrual Period (LMP). This is used by most healthcare providers worldwide.

The Three Trimesters

  • First Trimester (Weeks 1-12): Critical organ development. Morning sickness is common. The embryo becomes a fetus.
  • Second Trimester (Weeks 13-27): Often called the 'golden trimester.' Energy returns, baby movements begin (quickening), and sex can usually be determined.
  • Third Trimester (Weeks 28-40): Rapid fetal growth and preparation for delivery. Prenatal visits become more frequent.

Important Note

Only about 5% of babies are born on their exact due date. Most arrive within 2 weeks before or after. Your OB or midwife may adjust your due date after an early ultrasound for more accuracy.

macro-calculator.seoH1: Macro Calculator: Find Your Perfect Protein, Carb & Fat Split

faq.question: How many grams of protein do I need per day?
faq.answer: For muscle preservation and growth, research supports 0.7-1g of protein per pound of body weight (1.6-2.2g per kg). Higher isn't necessarily better — excess protein is used for energy, not extra muscle.

faq.question: Are carbs bad for weight loss?
faq.answer: No. Weight loss is driven by a calorie deficit, not by avoiding carbs. Low-carb diets work because they help some people eat fewer calories, not because carbs are inherently fattening.

faq.question: What macro split should I use for muscle gain?
faq.answer: A classic muscle-building split is 30% protein / 50% carbs / 20% fat. The most important factor is being in a slight calorie surplus (200-300 calories above TDEE) and hitting your protein target.

faq.question: What are the best protein sources for hitting my macro targets?
faq.answer: Chicken breast, fish (tuna, salmon), egg whites, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, and protein powder are lean, high-protein options. Red meat and whole eggs also provide protein but have more fat.

faq.question: How do I track my macros?
faq.answer: Apps like MyFitnessPal, Cronometer, or MacroFactor allow you to log food by scanning barcodes or searching a database. Weigh your food with a kitchen scale for accuracy, especially when starting.

macro-calculator.content:
Understanding Macronutrients

Macronutrients are the three main categories of nutrients that provide energy: protein, carbohydrates, and fats. Each plays a distinct role in body composition, performance, and health. Getting your macro split right is the foundation of evidence-based nutrition.

Why Macros Matter More Than Calories Alone

  • Protein (4 kcal/g): Preserves and builds muscle tissue. Crucial during fat loss to prevent 'skinny fat' results. Target: 0.8-1.2g per pound of body weight.
  • Carbohydrates (4 kcal/g): Primary energy source. Fuel performance in the gym and daily activities. Not the enemy — timing and quantity matter.
  • Fat (9 kcal/g): Essential for hormone production, brain function, and fat-soluble vitamin absorption. Never go below 0.3g per pound of body weight.

IIFYM — If It Fits Your Macros

The flexible dieting approach (IIFYM) allows eating any food as long as it fits within your daily macro targets. This improves long-term adherence compared to rigid restrictive diets.

ideal-weight-calculator.description: Calculate your ideal body weight using 4 clinical formulas including Hamwi, Devine, Robinson, and Miller.

ideal-weight-calculator.seoDescription: Find your ideal weight using 4 clinical formulas (Hamwi, Devine, Robinson, Miller). Calculate BMI healthy weight range and see results in kg and lbs. Free online weight calculator.
ideal-weight-calculator.seoKeywords: ideal weight calculator, ideal body weight, how much should I weigh, healthy weight calculator, ideal weight for height, Hamwi formula, Devine formula, BMI weight range, body weight calculator
ideal-weight-calculator.seoH1: Ideal Weight Calculator: Find Your Healthy Weight Range

faq.question: How much should I weigh for my height?
faq.answer: Healthy weight ranges vary by formula. The BMI healthy range (18.5–24.9) is widely used: for a 5'7" (170cm) person, this is approximately 54–80 kg. Our calculator provides 4 formula estimates and the BMI range.

faq.question: Why do the different formulas give different results?
faq.answer: Each formula was developed in different decades and for different medical purposes. They use slightly different base weights and increments per inch above 5 feet. The average of all four is the most balanced estimate.

faq.question: Is ideal weight the same for men and women of the same height?
faq.answer: No. Men generally have more muscle mass and bone density than women. All four formulas account for this biological difference with separate starting weights and multipliers.

faq.question: What if I'm naturally heavier than my ideal weight?
faq.answer: Ideal weight formulas are guidelines, not verdicts. Focus on health markers: blood pressure, cholesterol, blood sugar, and fitness level. Weight alone does not determine health.

faq.question: Can I be overweight by BMI but healthy?
faq.answer: Yes. Athletes with high muscle mass often have a BMI in the 'overweight' range despite having low body fat. This is a known limitation of BMI. Body fat percentage and waist circumference are better indicators.

ideal-weight-calculator.content:
What is Ideal Body Weight?

Ideal body weight (IBW) is a clinical estimate of the most healthy weight for a given height and gender. It is used by healthcare professionals for drug dosing, anesthesia, ventilator settings, and general health benchmarking. However, it is not a one-size-fits-all answer.

The 4 Clinical Formulas Compared

  • Hamwi (1964): The oldest and most commonly referenced. Used in clinical settings worldwide.
  • Devine (1974): Originally developed for medication dosing. Widely used in pharmacology.
  • Robinson (1983): A modified Devine formula, producing slightly different results for each gender.
  • Miller (1983): Generally produces the lowest (lightest) estimate of the four formulas.

IBW vs. BMI vs. Body Fat %

IBW formulas don't account for muscle mass or body composition. A muscular athlete may weigh more than their IBW while having an excellent health profile. Body fat percentage is a more accurate health indicator than weight alone.

faq.question: What is prompt engineering?
faq.answer: Prompt engineering is the practice of crafting precise, detailed instructions for AI language models to produce the best possible outputs. Good prompts include context, specific instructions, a defined role, and output format requirements.

faq.question: How do I make ChatGPT give better responses?
faq.answer: Be specific about role, task, context, format, and length. Use 'Act as a [expert role]', specify the audience, give examples of the format you want, and ask it to explain its reasoning.

faq.question: Can I use these prompts for free?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free to use and customize. These are template prompts you copy and paste into any AI — no subscription required for this generator.

faq.question: Do these prompts work with Claude and Gemini?
faq.answer: Yes. While prompts may need minor tweaks, the structural principles of good prompting are universal across all major AI models including ChatGPT, Claude (Anthropic), Gemini (Google), and Mistral.

faq.question: What is the difference between zero-shot and few-shot prompting?
faq.answer: Zero-shot prompting gives the AI only the instruction. Few-shot prompting includes 1-3 examples of the desired output style before the actual request. Few-shot prompting dramatically improves consistency and quality.

chatgpt-prompt-generator.content:
What Makes a Great AI Prompt?

A well-engineered prompt is the difference between a generic, mediocre AI response and a highly specific, actionable output that saves you hours of work. Prompt engineering is the skill of crafting instructions that guide AI models to produce exactly what you need.

The 5 Elements of a High-Quality Prompt

  1. Role: Tell the AI who to be ("Act as a senior marketing strategist").
  2. Task: Clearly define what you want ("Write a product description").
  3. Context: Provide relevant background ("Target audience: busy parents, age 30-45").
  4. Format: Specify the output structure ("200 words, bullet points, professional tone").
  5. Constraints: Set boundaries ("Do NOT use jargon. Include a clear CTA").

Works with All Major AI Models

Our prompts are designed to work with ChatGPT (GPT-4, GPT-4o), Claude (Anthropic), Gemini (Google), and Llama. Simply select a template, customize the placeholders, and paste into your preferred AI.

faq.question: How accurate is AI content detection?
faq.answer: Heuristic-based detectors are approximately 60-80% accurate on unedited AI text. The accuracy drops significantly on human-edited AI content or very short samples. No tool is foolproof.

faq.question: Can AI-generated text be edited to pass detection?
faq.answer: Yes. Humanizing AI text by adding personal pronouns, contractions, anecdotes, irregular sentence structures, and informal language significantly reduces AI detection scores.

faq.question: Is this tool free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free. All processing happens in your browser — your text is never sent to a server or stored anywhere.

faq.question: What AI models does this detect?
faq.answer: The patterns detected are common across all major LLMs including ChatGPT (GPT-4, GPT-4o), Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral, and others. The patterns are LLM-agnostic.

faq.question: Why did my clearly human-written text get a high AI score?
faq.answer: Formal, academic, or business writing often mimics AI patterns. If you write in a very structured, impersonal style, detection scores may be high despite the text being human-written. This is a known false positive.

ai-content-detector.content:
How Does AI Content Detection Work?

AI language models have characteristic writing patterns that differ from human writing. Our AI Content Detector uses heuristic analysis — matching text against known patterns, phrases, and linguistic features associated with AI-generated content.

Key Detection Signals

  • AI Phrases: LLMs frequently use transitions like 'it is worth noting', 'furthermore', 'in conclusion', and 'delve into'.
  • Passive Voice: AI models often default to passive constructions at higher rates than human writers.
  • Sentence Uniformity: AI tends to produce consistently structured sentences of similar lengths.
  • Lack of Personal Pronouns: Human writing contains 'I', 'me', 'my' and personal anecdotes more frequently.
  • Contractions: Humans naturally use contractions (can't, won't, I'm) more than AI models.

Limitations of AI Detection

No AI detector is 100% accurate. Edited AI content, text written in a formal academic style, or non-native English may trigger false positives. Use as a guideline, not a definitive verdict.

Create Realistic Handwriting from Any Text

Our Text to Handwriting Converter uses authentic Google handwriting fonts rendered on an HTML5 Canvas to produce realistic, downloadable handwriting images. It's perfect for creating personal-looking notes, artistic content, or school assignment aesthetics.

faq.question: Is this grammar checker free?
faq.answer: Yes, 100% free with no word limits, no signup required, and no watermarks. Processing happens entirely in your browser.

faq.question: What's the difference between a grammar checker and spellchecker?
faq.answer: A spellchecker catches misspelled words. A grammar checker understands context — it catches grammatically incorrect constructions, wrong word choices (their vs. there), and punctuation errors that spellcheckers miss.

faq.question: Can a grammar checker replace a human editor?
faq.answer: No. Automated grammar checkers catch common patterns but miss nuanced issues like tone, logical flow, and context-specific errors. Use it as a first pass, then have a human review important writing.

faq.question: How is this different from Grammarly?
faq.answer: Grammarly is a comprehensive paid service with an AI engine. Our tool is a free, open, browser-based rule checker. It's great for quick checks and catching common mistakes without needing an account.

faq.question: Does the grammar checker work for British English?
faq.answer: The rules are primarily based on American English conventions. British spellings (colour, favour) won't be flagged as errors, but some idiomatic differences may be detected.

grammar-checker.content:
Why Correct Grammar Matters

Grammar errors undermine credibility, confuse readers, and can cost you professionally. Whether you're writing a cover letter, an email to a client, or a blog post, correct grammar builds trust and authority. Our free grammar checker helps you catch the most common mistakes before they reach your audience.

Common Grammar Mistakes We Catch

  • Contraction Errors: 'dont' → 'don't', 'wont' → 'won't'
  • Verb Agreement: 'should of' → 'should have'
  • Homophone Confusion: 'their is' → 'there is', 'your welcome' → 'you're welcome'
  • Common Misspellings: 'definately' → 'definitely', 'recieve' → 'receive'
  • Punctuation: Missing commas after conjunctive adverbs

When to Use a Grammar Checker

Before sending any important email, submitting writing, or publishing online content. Even professional writers use grammar tools as a final check — it's not a sign of weakness but of diligence.

faq.question: Do hashtags help TikTok videos go viral?
faq.answer: Hashtags help the algorithm categorize your content and show it to people interested in those topics. They support virality but don't guarantee it — content quality and watch time are the primary drivers.

faq.question: Should I use #fyp on every TikTok?
faq.answer: You can, but it's not a magic button. The #fyp tag has billions of posts. Pair it with 2-3 niche-specific hashtags to ensure your content reaches the right sub-community.

faq.question: How many hashtags should I use on TikTok?
faq.answer: TikTok officially recommends 3-5 relevant hashtags. Creators who use 3-7 targeted hashtags generally see the best results. Avoid using 20+ hashtags as this can look like spam.

faq.question: Are trending hashtags better than niche hashtags?
faq.answer: For discoverability, niche hashtags are often better. A trending hashtag has millions of competing videos, making it hard to stand out. A niche hashtag with 100K posts has far less competition.

faq.question: When should I update my hashtags?
faq.answer: Refresh your hashtag strategy every 2-4 weeks. Monitor TikTok's Discover page and trend reports for emerging hashtags in your niche. What's trending changes weekly.

tiktok-hashtag-generator.content:
How TikTok Hashtags Work in 2024

TikTok's algorithm uses hashtags to understand your content and distribute it to interested users. Unlike Instagram and Twitter, TikTok's primary discovery mechanism is the For You Page (FYP), which is driven by engagement signals — but hashtags help bootstrap distribution to the right audience.

The Best TikTok Hashtag Strategy

  • Use 3-5 hashtags, not 30: More isn't better. Overstuffed captions look spammy and dilute your signal.
  • Mix tiers: 1-2 mega-tags (#fyp, millions of posts), 2-3 niche tags (thousands of posts), 1 trending tag.
  • #fyp and #foryou work, but aren't magic: They're signals, not guarantees. Quality content is the primary driver.
  • Niche hashtags win: A smaller, targeted hashtag (#veganfitness vs #fitness) puts you in front of more relevant viewers.

Category-Specific Strategy

Each content niche has different high-performing tags. Entertainment content dominates with broad FYP tags, while niche communities like gaming and fitness respond better to specific community hashtags.

faq.question: How much does YouTube pay per 1,000 views?
faq.answer: YouTube pays $0.01 to $0.03 per view on average, or $1 to $30 per 1,000 views (RPM). The actual amount depends heavily on your niche, audience location, ad types, and video length.

faq.question: How many views do you need to make $1,000 on YouTube?
faq.answer: With an average RPM of $3, you'd need approximately 333,000 views to earn $1,000. With a high-RPM niche like finance ($15 RPM), you'd only need about 67,000 views.

faq.question: What is the difference between CPM and RPM on YouTube?
faq.answer: CPM (Cost Per Mille) is what advertisers pay per 1,000 impressions. RPM is what creators actually receive per 1,000 views after YouTube takes its 45% cut. Always use RPM to estimate your actual earnings.

faq.question: How many subscribers do you need to monetize YouTube?
faq.answer: To join the YouTube Partner Program (YPP), you need 1,000 subscribers AND 4,000 watch hours in the past 12 months (or 1,000 subscribers and 10 million Shorts views).

faq.question: What YouTube niche pays the most?
faq.answer: Finance and investing ($10-30 RPM), legal/attorney ($15-25 RPM), software/SaaS ($9-18 RPM), and real estate ($8-15 RPM) have the highest RPMs. Entertainment, gaming, and vlogs are typically $2-5 RPM.

youtube-money-calculator.content:
How YouTube Pays Creators

YouTube pays creators through AdSense based on the number of monetized views and the RPM (Revenue Per Mille) — the actual revenue earned per 1,000 views after YouTube's 45% cut. RPM is the real-world metric, while CPM is what advertisers pay before the revenue split.

Why RPM Varies So Much

  • Niche: Finance, legal, and B2B videos command $10-30+ RPM. Entertainment and gaming typically see $2-5 RPM.
  • Geography: US, UK, Canada, and Australia viewers are worth 5-10x more than viewers in developing countries.
  • Seasonality: Q4 (October-December) has the highest ad spending — RPM can be 2-3x higher than Q1.
  • Video length: Videos over 8 minutes can include mid-roll ads, significantly increasing revenue per view.

Revenue Beyond AdSense

Smart creators diversify their income: YouTube memberships, Super Chats, merchandise, sponsorships, and affiliate marketing often make up the majority of a successful channel's income.

faq.question: How do I edit my crontab on Linux?
faq.answer: Run 'crontab -e' in your terminal to open the crontab editor. Add your cron expressions (one per line), save, and the cron daemon will automatically pick up the new schedule.

faq.question: How do I test a cron expression without waiting?
faq.answer: Use the 'at' command for one-time scheduled testing, or temporarily set the cron to run every minute (* * * * *) to verify it works, then update to the desired schedule.

faq.question: Why is my cron job not running?
faq.answer: Common causes: incorrect syntax, environment variables not set (cron runs with a minimal PATH), script not executable (chmod +x), output not redirected (errors are silent), or cron daemon not running (systemctl status cron).

faq.question: What timezone does cron use?
faq.answer: By default, cron uses the system timezone. On many servers this is UTC. Always verify with 'timedatectl' or 'date' to avoid unexpected scheduling discrepancies.

faq.question: Can I run a cron job every 30 seconds?
faq.answer: The minimum cron interval is 1 minute. For sub-minute execution, use a loop inside the cron script: * * * * * /path/to/script.sh; sleep 30; /path/to/script.sh

crontab-generator.content:
What is a Cron Job?

A cron job is a scheduled task in Unix/Linux systems that runs automatically at specified intervals. Defined in the crontab (cron table) file, they're used for automated backups, report generation, database maintenance, API calls, and server monitoring.

The Cron Expression Format

A cron expression has 5 fields: MINUTE HOUR DAY MONTH WEEKDAY. Each field accepts specific values and wildcards:

  • * — any value (every minute, every hour, etc.)
  • */n — every n units (*/5 = every 5 minutes)
  • a,b — multiple values (1,15 = 1st and 15th)
  • a-b — range (1-5 = Monday through Friday)

Common Cron Job Use Cases

  • 0 0 * * * — Daily backup at midnight
  • */5 * * * * — API health check every 5 minutes
  • 0 9 * * 1-5 — Daily report email every weekday at 9am
  • 0 0 1 * * — Monthly invoice generation

Privacy.section5Text: We may update this policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated modification date.
